content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

All three amendments identify real reconciliation defects in the terminal final-review propose-new-bead path.

## Votes

- `fr-5-0b120d63`: Accept
  The active bead is closed before terminal reconciliation, and the ownership check excludes closed statuses. That can route current-bead work into follow-up records, violating the fix-current-bead-first policy. Checking the active bead independently of terminal status is technically warranted.
- `fr-5-49275fe9`: Accept
  Threshold-met terminal proposals currently get only an ndjson handoff record, while below-threshold proposals are journaled. Adding an idempotent structured journal event for the created proposal improves auditability and keeps terminal reconciliation consistent with the rest of the propose-new-bead flow.
- `fr-5-bc97dd0b`: Accept
  Final review already preserves authoritative proposed title, scope, severity, and rationale, but terminal reconciliation drops them and falls back to summary/body text plus unknown severity. That weakens matching and produces lower-quality journal/proposal records, so carrying the richer metadata through is correct.
